summary:
Temporomandibular joint ankylosis can structurally alter jaw positioning leading to potential airway obstructions associated with OSA; whereas a mandibular tumor or cysts are less directly related as primary causes for this condition due their location away from major respiratory pathways involved in breathing while asleep.
The summary focuses on how TMJ ankylosis affects jaw positioning which leads to possible airway blockage compared to tumors/cysts being farther removed anatomically impacting respiration significantly when sleeping. 

-
